title: IaP Compiler Operation Batch v1
description: Schema for a proposal batch (apiVersion operations.iap.dev/v1) per IEP-0009: an ordered list of enveloped compiler operations, applied transactionally to an authored IaP document by the operation gate. The operation vocabulary is closed in v1 (twelve types); unknown types fail structural validation instead of entering YAML. Envelopes carry identity, confidence, assumptions, required clarifications, and provenance; validationResult and previewDiff are gate OUTPUTS that MAY be echoed back on a stored or replayed envelope — the gate always recomputes both and never trusts supplied values. Update-shaped operations carry explicit change.set / change.unset lists (phase-3 design decision 2): no merge-patch ambiguity. This schema is a structural pre-filter used for structured-output enforcement against model adapters; the document schema (iap-v1.schema.json) remains the authority for the resulting document, enforced by the gate's transactional dry-run.

operations.description: The ordered operations of this proposal. Application is sequential and transactional: any operation failing validation aborts the whole batch and leaves the document untouched (IEP-0009 rule 1). An empty proposal is meaningless and is rejected.

resourceId.description: DNS-label grammar shared with the document schema: resource identifiers, profile names, and policy ids (spec ch. 2 §2.6.1).

namespaceName.description: Extension namespace grammar (spec ch. 11 §11.1). Namespaces are flat; SetExtensionValue is scoped to exactly one namespace.

kindName.description: The closed v1 kind vocabulary (core plus reserved registry kinds, spec ch. 3/ch. 5). A hallucinated kind fails here, structurally, before any YAML exists.

dotPath.description: Dot path with non-empty segments (e.g. spec.capacity.storage); array elements by zero-based index. Paths are relative to the construct the operation targets. Keys containing a literal dot are not addressable in v1.

changeSetUnset.description: Explicit update payload (phase-3 design decision 2, resolving IEP-0009 open question 1): set maps target-relative dot paths to their new values; unset lists target-relative dot paths to remove. At least one of the two is required (minProperties); no merge-patch semantics exist.

set.description: Map of target-relative dot paths to the exact values to write. Missing intermediate objects are created; writing through an existing non-container value is refused by the gate.

unset.description: Target-relative dot paths to remove. Removing an absent path is an idempotent no-op.

labels.description: Free-form key/value labels; values are always strings (mirrors the document schema).

relationshipEdge.description: Inline edge payload (spec ch. 4). The operation's target resource is always the edge source.

type.description: Relationship verb; the v1 verb set is closed (spec ch. 4 §4.3).

target.description: Resource identifier of the edge target; must resolve in the document at the point this operation applies.

port.description: Target port asserted by this edge.

protocol.description: Wire protocol asserted by this edge.

access.description: Access level asserted by this edge (connectsTo/storesDataIn only).

relationshipRef.description: Identifies one existing inline edge on the target resource by its (verb, target) pair. Resolution refuses when no edge matches (dangling-target) or more than one matches (ambiguous-target); verb or target changes are expressed as remove + create, never as an update.

type.description: Relationship verb of the edge being addressed.

target.description: Resource identifier the edge points at.

policyCondition.description: Policy condition tree (spec ch. 7 §7.3): a {field, operator, value} leaf or an allOf/anyOf/not combinator over further conditions.

oneOf.description: Condition leaf: a dot path from the resource entry root, a closed operator, and an operand.

field.description: Dot path from the resource entry root (e.g. spec.encryption.atRest).

operator.description: Comparison operator; the v1 operator set is closed (spec ch. 7 §7.4).

value.description: Operand for the operator; omitted for exists/absent.

oneOf.description: Conjunction: every member condition must hold.

allOf.description: Member conditions, all of which must hold.

oneOf.description: Disjunction: at least one member condition must hold.

anyOf.description: Member conditions, at least one of which must hold.

finding.description: One machine-readable validation finding in the ch. 8 §8.6 format, produced by the deterministic pipeline during the gate's dry run. IaP codes pass through untouched.

severity.description: error invalidates the document; warning never does (ch. 8 §8.2).

path.description: Dot path from the document root to the offending value.

message.description: Human-readable explanation; not intended for programmatic matching.

policyId.description: The violated policy's id (phase 5/7 findings only).

envelope.description: One enveloped operation: the typed change request plus the metadata that makes AI proposals inspectable, individually validated, replayable, and model-independent (IEP-0009).

operationId.description: Caller-supplied operation identity, unique within the batch; cited by operation-log entries, confirmations, and per-field provenance records (OP-4). Never generated from ambient state.

type.description: The operation type; the v1 vocabulary is closed at exactly these twelve (IEP-0009). Unknown types are rejected structurally.

target.description: What the operation addresses; the required members depend on the operation type (see the per-type branches of this schema).

resourceId.description: Resource identifier being created, changed, or removed; for relationship operations, the edge SOURCE; for resource-level SetExtensionValue, the refined resource.

profile.description: Profile name being applied or removed (ApplyProfile/RemoveProfile).

policyId.description: Policy id being added or changed (AddPolicy/ChangeConstraint).

namespace.description: Extension namespace being written (SetExtensionValue only); the operation is scoped to exactly this namespace (ch. 11 non-interference).

relationship.description: Existing edge being updated or removed (UpdateRelationship/RemoveRelationship).

change.description: The operation payload; its shape is constrained per operation type by the branches below. Remove-type operations carry no change.

sourceSpan.description: Where in the source input this operation was extracted from; enables review UIs to highlight the originating text.

input.description: Identifier of the source input (e.g. a natural-language request id).

start.description: Zero-based start offset (inclusive) within the source input.

end.description: Zero-based end offset (exclusive) within the source input.

text.description: The quoted source text, for display.

confidence.description: Extraction confidence in [0, 1]. Below the session threshold (default 0.8, phase-3 design decision 2 / IEP-0009 open question 3) the operation cannot commit without a recorded confirmation (OP-3).

assumptions.description: Values the proposer assumed rather than extracted. ANY non-empty list forces human confirmation before commit (IEP-0009 rule 2); an empty array is an explicit claim that nothing was assumed.

items.description: One assumed value and why it was assumed.

field.description: Target-relative dot path of the assumed field.

reason.description: Why the value was assumed rather than asked for.

requiredClarifications.description: Open questions that block this operation. ANY non-empty list forces human confirmation before commit (IEP-0009 rule 2); the deterministic clarification engine (roadmap §3.3, M3.3) populates these.

id.description: Stable question identifier, referenced by the answering confirmation.

question.description: The question, phrased for a human.

field.description: Target-relative dot path of the field the question blocks.

provenance.description: Where this proposal came from. The source vocabulary is closed (phase-3 design decision 6); mapping-default is reserved — mapping defaults exist right of the boundary and never flow back.

source.description: Closed provenance source vocabulary (phase-3 design decision 6, OP-4).

channel.description: The authoring surface that produced the proposal (IEP-0009 summary: natural language, guided UI, IDE commands, visual designer; api covers programmatic callers).

modelId.description: Identifier of the model/adapter that produced the proposal, for the audit log. Never influences application: the same confirmed batch produces the same document regardless of this value (OP-2).

promptVersion.description: Version of the prompt artifact used, for the audit log (M3.4 prompt registry).

validationResult.description: Gate OUTPUT, optionally echoed back on stored/replayed envelopes: the dry-run validation outcome. The gate recomputes this on every apply and never trusts a supplied value.

status.description: pass when the dry run produced no error-severity findings.

findings.description: The dry run's findings, in the ch. 8 §8.6 machine-readable format.

previewDiff.description: Gate OUTPUT, optionally echoed back on stored/replayed envelopes: the semantic preview diff. The gate recomputes this on every apply and never trusts a supplied value.

format.description: Preview diff format identifier; always iap-semantic-diff/v1.

adds.description: Document paths added by the change (a whole added subtree reports its root path only).

removes.description: Document paths removed by the change.

changes.description: Document paths whose canonical value changed.

destructive.description: True when the change removes a stateful resource or touches a replacement-eligible field (ch. 14 §14.2, phase-3 design decision 8); destructive changes require explicit acknowledgment in the confirmation.

destructiveOperations.description: The operations that made the diff destructive, with machine-readable reasons.

reason.description: stateful-remove: RemoveResource on a stateful kind; replace-eligible-update: an update touching a replacement-eligible field per the kind's ch. 3 lifecycle rules.

paths.description: The touched replacement-eligible paths (empty for stateful-remove).

type.description: Branch discriminator: applies when the operation type is CreateResource.

target.description: CreateResource target: the identifier of the resource to create.

resourceId.description: Identifier for the new resource; must not already exist.

change.description: The complete resource entry to create. extensions is deliberately absent: SetExtensionValue is the sole path into extensions (ch. 11).

kind.description: The new resource's kind (closed v1 vocabulary).

spec.description: Kind-specific intent fields; the document schema validates the content during the gate's dry run.

relationships.description: Inline edges declared with the resource; the new resource is the source. Edge targets must resolve at the point this operation applies.

type.description: Branch discriminator: applies when the operation type is UpdateResource.

target.description: UpdateResource target: the resource being updated.

resourceId.description: Identifier of the existing resource to update.

change.description: Explicit set/unset payload with target-relative dot paths (design decision 2).

type.description: Branch discriminator: applies when the operation type is RemoveResource.

target.description: RemoveResource target: the resource being removed.

resourceId.description: Identifier of the existing resource to remove.

type.description: Branch discriminator: applies when the operation type is CreateRelationship.

target.description: CreateRelationship target: the SOURCE resource of the new edge.

resourceId.description: Identifier of the existing resource that declares the edge (the edge source).

change.description: The complete inline edge to add; the target resource is the source.

type.description: Branch discriminator: applies when the operation type is UpdateRelationship.

target.description: UpdateRelationship target: the edge source resource plus the (verb, target) pair identifying the edge.

resourceId.description: Identifier of the resource that declares the edge (the edge source).

relationship.description: The (verb, target) pair identifying the edge on the source resource.

change.description: Explicit set/unset payload with target-relative dot paths (design decision 2).

type.description: Branch discriminator: applies when the operation type is RemoveRelationship.

target.description: RemoveRelationship target: the edge source resource plus the (verb, target) pair identifying the edge.

resourceId.description: Identifier of the resource that declares the edge (the edge source).

relationship.description: The (verb, target) pair identifying the edge on the source resource.

type.description: Branch discriminator: applies when the operation type is ApplyProfile.

target.description: ApplyProfile target: the profile name to create or wholly replace (upsert; profiles are complete definitions, not patches of patches).

profile.description: Name of the profile definition being written.

extends.description: Name of one other profile whose overrides are applied first.

overrides.description: RFC 7386 JSON Merge Patch applied to the document when this profile is active.

type.description: Branch discriminator: applies when the operation type is RemoveProfile.

target.description: RemoveProfile target: the profile definition being removed.

profile.description: Name of the existing profile definition to remove.

type.description: Branch discriminator: applies when the operation type is AddPolicy.

target.description: AddPolicy target: the id of the policy being added; must not already exist.

policyId.description: Identifier for the new policy (written as the policy's id).

change.description: The policy body (spec ch. 7); the id comes from target.policyId. target is required explicitly — the gate materializes no implicit targeting.

target.description: What the policy targets: kinds and/or a label selector; {} targets every resource (ch. 7 §7.2).

selector.description: Label selector narrowing the targeted resources.

labels.description: Labels a resource must carry to match.

rule.description: The policy condition tree the effect applies to (ch. 7 §7.3).

effect.description: deny: violation fails validation; warn: violation is reported; require: the rule MUST hold (ch. 7 §7.5).

params.description: Named scalar parameters referenced by the rule.

type.description: Branch discriminator: applies when the operation type is ChangeConstraint.

target.description: ChangeConstraint target: the existing policy whose constraint is being changed.

policyId.description: Identifier of the existing policy to change.

change.description: Explicit set/unset payload with target-relative dot paths (design decision 2).

type.description: Branch discriminator: applies when the operation type is SetMetadata.

target.description: SetMetadata target: the document metadata block is singular, so the target carries no members.

change.description: Explicit set/unset payload with target-relative dot paths (design decision 2).

type.description: Branch discriminator: applies when the operation type is SetExtensionValue.

target.description: SetExtensionValue target: the namespace being written, and the resource whose extensions block is refined (omitted for document-level registration).

namespace.description: The extension namespace this operation is scoped to; paths in the change are relative to extensions.<namespace> and structurally cannot escape it.

resourceId.description: Resource whose extensions.<namespace> block is refined; omitted for the document-level registration block.

change.description: Explicit set/unset payload with target-relative dot paths (design decision 2).
